data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0010/sessions/0040-horizon-T12

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground orientation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session. Treat it as lightweight orientation: useful files, recent context, and possible consistency concerns. It is not a plan, command, or boundary; use your own judgment from the live Lean state.
- Useful context: the generic-flatness cone is now axiom-clean (`AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness`, `genericFlatnessAlgebraic`, domain core `genericallyFree_quotient_prime_of_fibre_dim_le`, `exists_generic_rank_comparison` — all `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`). `FlatteningStratification.lean` holds exactly 3 geometric `sorry`s: `flatLocusStratification` (n=0, ~1475), `flatLocusReduction` (~1520), `flatteningStratification_universal` (~1668); `flatLocusAssembly`/`flatteningStratification`/`.ofCurve` are derived.

- Relevant files: `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean` (sorry-free home for geometric glue; keeps Mathlib-only imports so `FlatteningStratification`'s dévissage instances stay stable — importing `QuotScheme` there perturbed those proofs). `flatLocusReduction` needs Noetherian well-founded recursion on closed subsets (`NoetherianSpace`), reduced structure via `IdealSheafData.radical`/`subscheme`, and per-component generic flatness; Mathlib's `IdealSheaf/IrreducibleComponent.lean` covers the reduced-subscheme-on-irreducible-components gap.

- Blueprint/consistency: `Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex` matches the Lean — new node `lem:generic_rank_comparison` (complete proof, `\leanok`), proof-`\leanok` on the domain core and both flatness theorems, and the finite-strata nodes now say "noetherian" (repair of a false `IsLocallyNoetherian` statement; ⊔ₙ𝔸ⁿ nested-flag counterexample in the docstring + a blueprint remark). DAG 2201 nodes, 0 dangling; `lem:generic_rank_comparison` reads as isolated only because its `\uses` are proof-level (`I-0051`).

- Build/ops: at load ~2, `lake build` of the two modules ran green in well under a minute (8562 jobs). Memory files `r3-domain-core-kernel-walls.md`, `flattening-stratification-repaired-devissage.md`, and inbox `I-0092` carry the reusable r5 recipes (clean-binder extraction, `maxSynthPendingDepth`, `letI` tower registration, the `inferInstance`-pinning trap). QuotScheme's own 3 base-change leaves (`I-0089`) remain flatness-gated on `AJC.fbc`/T2.

# Task focus
Task: T12
Title: Picard scheme representability (FGA) — Pic_{C/k} representable, locally of finite type
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean
References: roadmap=AJC.picrep

Prove **representability of the relative Picard functor by a scheme** (FGA / Grothendieck FGA 232 / Theorem 4.8 in Kleiman) — roadmap cone `AJC.picrep`. For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C/k$ with a $k$-rational point, the relative Picard functor is representable by a $k$-scheme locally of finite type:

$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable by a scheme, locally of finite type}.$$

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC — axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
(no accepted inbox items)

# Memory
- **leandag audit convention: the DAG engine registers only STATEMENT-level `\uses`; proof-level `\uses` are ignored.**

- Consequence: a blueprint node whose only dependencies are declared inside its proof block shows as **isolated** in `horizon leandag`, even though the math dependency is real and the proof is complete. This is expected, not a blueprint defect.
- Observed run 0006 (Albanese): 297 nodes, **178 isolated**, but **0 dangling** and 287 proved — the high isolated count is entirely this artifact.
- **The health metric to trust is `dangling` (should be 0) and `proved`, NOT `isolated`.** Do not chase isolated nodes as missing edges; if you want them linked in the DAG, lift the relevant `\uses` from the proof block up to the statement line.
- Same engine limitation was noted by the T6 Horizon session; recorded here so future Ground audits don't misread it.
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements — Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman §4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.
- `instPicSharpRepresentable` proved by `choose_spec`; `groupSchemeStructure : CommGrpObj (PicScheme C)` proved via `CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Multiplicative.ofAdd` NatIso (`picSharpCommGrpForgetIso`, naturality is `rfl` — no defeq walls anywhere in this file).
- Consumers: quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` (synthesized from `[HasRationalPoint C]`) — `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` in `IdentityComponent.lean` now carry it. `Pic0Scheme C := GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` is now a real def (axiom-clean), gated on `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType`.

**Caveat 1 — pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **Stacks 01BG general pullback-qcoh CLOSED + FBC RHS tilde leaf CLOSED (run 0003 session 0013, T2) — supersedes all "general-morphism pullback qcoh is absent / only in sorry-tainted QuotScheme" notes.**

- `Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean` (new, ~185 LOC, axiom-clean): `pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom (g : Y ⟶ X)` for ARBITRARY scheme morphisms. Recipe: localize the pullback, not the presentation — per cover member `A`, `(g^*F)|_{g⁻¹A} ≅ (g.resLE A W)^*(F|_A)` via `pullbackComp`+`pullbackCongr (resLE_comp_ι g le_rfl)`+`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, transport the global presentation of `F|_A` by `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` (15-underscore @-application, copy `presentationRestrictSliceOfOver`'s shape), then the CechTermAcyclic over-bridge tail (`modulesOverOpensEquivalence` / `overOpensIsoRestrict` / `overOpensFunctorUnitIso` / counit) and `IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop` on the preimage cover.
- Enablers now available: `opensMap_final` (`(Opens.map φ).Final` for ANY continuous map — structured-arrow posets are ⊔-directed, `Functor.final_of_isFiltered_structuredArrow`) and `pullbackUnitIso : g^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y` (Mathlib's `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` iso under `[F.Final]`). Gotcha: re-synthesis of `IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit …)` at USE sites can fail (defeq wall in the instance-arg unification) — pass the instance explicitly via `@asIso _ _ _ _ _ (pullbackObjUnitToUnit_isIso_hom g)`.
- FBC leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde` (622-line sorry) closed as literally `(pushforward f').mapIso (twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma …) ≪≫ pushPullObj_coverInter_pushforward_iso_tilde f' 𝒰' (g'^*F) (pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom g' F hF) σ` — the def had to MOVE below `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma` in the file. FBC leaves 5 → 4.
- Iteration cost: `lake env lean` on a file importing Mathlib+`CechTermAcyclic` ≈ 4 min under load ~65; the 2-module `lake build` (new file + `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`) ≈ 25 min.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.

- **`horizon commit` fails with an EMPTY error** in two cases: (a) no git identity — export `GIT_COMMITTER_NAME='Archon Horizon' GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL='archon-horizon@local'` (+ AUTHOR pair) first; (b) nothing to commit — the workspace ledger's end-of-run sweep (`workspace[NNNN] run finished`) may have already integrated files you think are stranded. Diagnose with `git --git-dir=.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git --work-tree=. diff HEAD --stat -- <project-path>`. Per-project journals under `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git` can lag several runs behind — that's the orchestrator's job.

At session start check `pgrep -af claude` + parent PIDs; terminate a same-project claude whose horizon runner is dead, but read its transcript tail first and inherit its pending intent (session 0011's uncommitted surgery was sound and became the session's main result).
- Recipe: building ModuleCat-R morphisms into sheaf-of-modules section objects (v4.31) — the ConcreteCategory.ofHom + presheaf-obj pattern, and the let-fvar IsIso gotcha

Landed in `tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen` (`QuotScheme.lean`, T12 run 0010). When you need `f : ModuleCat.of R M ⟶ <sections object>` (e.g. to feed `tilde.map`):

- **Target the honest `ModuleCat R` object** `(modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)` — this is literally the domain module of Mathlib's `fromTildeΓ`/`toOpen`. Do NOT target `moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G` via `ModuleCat.ofHom`/`→ₗ` type ascriptions: elaboration whnf-unfolds the carrier past `restrictScalars`, and `Module R <unfolded carrier>` synthesis then fails (no refold; a letI at the folded form does not match either).
- **Construct via `ConcreteCategory.ofHom { toFun := ..., map_add' := ..., map_smul' := ... }`** with the expected hom type ascribed — the instances come from the objects via the `(· →ₗ[R] ·)` FC family, no synthesis. A bare `let blin : M →ₗ[R] <object> := ...` fails; skipping the standalone LinearMap and going straight to the categorical hom works.
- **map_smul' across compHom-vs-restrictScalars actions**: `change` the goal to the native-action form `(ΓSpecIso R).inv.hom r • f x`, then `rw [← hact]; exact h1` where `hact : appLE V ⊤ e = (ΓSpecIso R).inv` (proved by `rw [Scheme.Hom.appLE, fromSpec_app_self, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, Subsingleton.elim _ (𝟙 ⊤), op_id, map_id, comp_id]`).
- **IsIso of `tilde.map b'` for a `let`-bound b'**: `haveI : IsIso (tilde.map b') := by rw [show tilde.map b' = (tilde.functor _).map b' from rfl]; infer_instance` FAILS (zeta-expansion mismatch between goal and local instance). Use `inferInstanceAs (IsIso ((tilde.functor _).map b'))` — works even without `clear_value`.
- **Unit-component bijectivity at open immersions**: `Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app (restrictAdjunction j) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction j) N` + `restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app` (plain presheaf restriction along `j''ᵁj⁻¹ᵁV = V`) + component of `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`. When `rw [← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply]` fails with "target not type-correct under instances transparency" (`Γ((𝟭 X.Modules).obj N, V)` forms), do `change`-restatement at the type-correct form, then close with `exact congrArg (fun φ => (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom φ) y) hcomp` where `hcomp : f ≫ g = 𝟙 _` — both sides defeq, no rewriting in the wall.
- **Σ-pair trace**: `tilde.toOpen_map_app` (naturality) + `toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app` at `⊤` (+ `homOfLE le_top = 𝟙 ⊤` by `Subsingleton.elim`) give `(tilde.map b' ≫ fromTildeΓ).app ⊤ ∘ toOpen = b'` elementwise, via `congrArg`-chains only.
- T12/QuotScheme state after run 0010 (Lane F transport layer CLOSED) — updated triage

The Lane F affine-section-formula chain in `QuotScheme.lean` is now **fully closed** — the run-0010 "next single-session targets" (01XJ `pushforward_isQuasicoherent`, N1 baseMap naturality, 01HQ `pullback_tildeIso`) are all done, axiom-clean.

- **QuotScheme sorries now 8** (was 11 → 10 → 8): the **5 headline** reps decls (`hilbertPolynomial`, `QuotFunctor`, `QuotScheme`, `Grassmannian`, `Grassmannian.representable` — all deep, Mathlib-v4.31-gapped) + **3 `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_*`** leaves. - **Closed this arc (all axiom-clean, `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`)**: `tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen` (01I8, via union-merge substrate), `pushforward_isQuasicoherent` (01XJ, MV-induction + `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`), `pullback_tildeIso` (01HQ, **uniqueness of left adjoints** through the new `pullbackTilde_gammaBridge` NatIso — see file-memory `pullback-tilde-01hq-closed.md`), and `pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv` + N1–N4 + `_isBaseChange` + `pullback_app_isoTensor` (Beck–Chevalley section formula).

The union-merge tail earlier in the file has sorry-free gap1/gap2 substrate (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent`, `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`); its blueprint nodes are still absent (`I-0087`).
- **Remaining real progress on `AJC.picrep` needs**: (1) T2/`AJC.fbc` flatness algebra (unblocks the 3 base-change leaves); (2) a Route-C coherent-$\chi$ / Riemann–Roch substrate (the 5 headline decls). Neither is single-session. See triage `I-0086`.
- **Recipes: module-level dévissage in Lean v4.31 (landed in FlatteningStratification GenericFreeness, run 0010 T12 r2) — reuse for any prime-filtration/SES argument.**

- **ModuleCat SES arguments need ONE universe**: `ModuleCat.ofHom {X Y : Type v}` forces all modules of a `ShortComplex (ModuleCat R)` into the same universe. Binders `{M₁ M₂ M₃ : Type*}` give distinct universes and produce baffling failures — instance goals displayed as raw `Quot ⇑(OreLocalization.oreEqv ...)` (LocalizedModule unfolded past its instance heads) plus universe-mismatched `Eq.{max u_1 u_4}` vs `Eq.{max u_1 u_2}`. Pin `Type v`. The dévissage eliminator is single-universe anyway.
- **`set S := Submonoid.powers (f*g)` breaks instance unification** in localized-module types (same family as the known let-fvar IsIso gotcha): instance search will not zeta-unfold the set-fvar when unifying `Localization S'` heads. Write the submonoid term explicitly everywhere; `let`-bound MAPS (data) are fine — only types/instances mind.
- **Quantified-instance motive** for `IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime` when the property lives over a DIFFERENT ring A than the induction ring B: `motive N _ iMB _ := ∀ (iA : Module A N), @IsScalarTower A B N Algebra.toSMul iMB.toDistribMulAction.toMulAction.toSMul iA.toDistribMulAction.toMulAction.toSMul → @P A N _ _ iA`. Cases: subsingleton via @-application; quotient case transports along `(e.restrictScalars A).symm` (canonical tower on B⧸p is instance-found); exact case builds `letI iA₁ := Module.compHom N₁ (algebraMap A B)` + tower `⟨fun a b n => by change (a • b) • n = algebraMap A B a • b • n; rw [Algebra.smul_def, mul_smul]⟩`; B-linear maps restrict via `LinearMap.restrictScalars A` and inj/surj/Exact transfer definitionally. Final application `... ‹Module A M› ‹IsScalarTower A B M›` unifies. Compiled essentially first-try.
- **Two-localization freeness transport** (`M_f` free ⟹ `M_{fg}` free) WITHOUT IsLocalizedModule-composition: `(IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayRight f g).toAlgebra` + `IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq (awayToAwayRight_eq ..).symm`, then equiv-chain `congr(refl, isBaseChange f) .symm ≪≫ cancelBaseChange ≪≫ isBaseChange (f*g)` and `Module.Free.tensor` + `Module.Free.of_equiv'`. Give every LinearEquiv a fully-typed `have` — `refine LinearEquiv.trans ?_ e` with open source type sticks on RingHomCompTriple metavariables.
- **Localized SES**: `IsLocalizedModule.map/map_injective/map_surjective/map_exact` at `mkLinearMap`s, upgrade R-linear→Loc-linear with `LinearMap.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization` (coe unchanged, so Function.Exact/Injective/Surjective transfer by defeq); `ShortComplex.mk (ofHom u') (ofHom v') (ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun x => hexact.apply_apply_eq_zero x))`; ShortExact via `moduleCat_exact_iff` + `mono_iff_injective` + `epi_iff_surjective`; finish `ModuleCat.free_shortExact`.
- **refine-under-binder**: `refine subsingleton_of_forall_eq 0 fun x => ?_` fails (?_ under lambda); use `apply` + `intro` + `induction x using LocalizedModule.induction_on with | _ m s => ...`. `Submonoid.mk_smul` bridges `(⟨c,hc⟩ : ↥S) • m` to `c • m`.

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action — never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, `## Why I stopped`, and `## Next`; add or rename other sections if another shape is clearer, but keep `## Why I stopped`. In that section, start with either `Task complete:` when the assigned objective is genuinely complete, or `Task not complete:` when work remains. If there is a plausible next action inside the session's scope and budget, take it before stopping; a clean commit or green partial check is not by itself a reason to stop. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this {role} session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

1380	/-! ## §1. Coherent-sheaf flatness over a base
1381	
1382	For a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` of schemes and a coherent (more generally:
1383	quasi-coherent) `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, the *coherent-sheaf flatness* predicate
1384	`CoherentSheafFlat f 𝓕` captures Stacks 00HB lifted to the sheaf level:
1385	for every point `x ∈ X` the stalk `𝓕_x` is a flat `𝓞_{S, f(x)}`-module
1386	via the composite `𝓞_{S, f(x)} → 𝓞_{X, x} → End(𝓕_x)`.
1387	
1388	Affine-locally (Stacks 00HB equivalence): on an affine open `U = Spec A
1389	⊆ S` and an affine open `V = Spec B ⊆ f⁻¹U` with `𝓕|_V = M̃` for a
1390	finite `B`-module `M`, the condition becomes `Module.Flat A M` with the
1391	`A`-module structure inherited via the ring map `A → B`. The
1392	file-internal encoding uses this affine-local form.
1393	
1394	Blueprint reference: `def:coherent_sheaf_flat`. -/
1395	
1396	/-- A coherent sheaf `𝓕` on `X` is **flat over `S`** for a morphism
1397	`f : X ⟶ S` if, for every affine open `U ⊆ S` and every affine open
1398	`V ⊆ f⁻¹U`, the `𝓞_X`-module of sections `Γ(𝓕, V)` is flat as a
1399	`Γ(S, U)`-module, where the `Γ(S, U)`-action is the restriction of
1400	scalars along the ring morphism `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` (the `appLE`
1401	morphism of `f`).
1402	
1403	This is the affine-local form of Stacks 00HB (a coherent sheaf is flat
1404	over the base if every stalk is flat over the corresponding base
1405	stalk); the affine-local form and stalk form are equivalent for
1406	*quasi-coherent* sheaves on a noetherian base, which is the regime of
1407	the flattening-stratification theorem.
1408	
1409	iter-177+: the body of subsequent flat-locus theorems may package this
1410	predicate as an instance-driven typeclass `Module.Flat`-style if the
1411	sheafified-tensor infrastructure lands in Mathlib; for the iter-176
1412	file-skeleton it is encoded as a `Prop` quantifier. -/
1413	def CoherentSheafFlat {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1414	  ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
1415	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
1416	    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V) := Module.compHom _ (f.appLE U V e).hom
1417	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V)
1418

1432	
1433	The Nitsure §4 proof of the existence theorem decomposes into three
1434	sub-lemmas wired together by Noetherian induction. The first is the
1435	special case `n = 0` (when `ℙⁿ_S = S`); the second is the
1436	Noetherian-induction reduction to a finite stratification where `𝓕`
1437	*already* becomes flat (but without polynomial indexing); the third is
1438	the assembly step that re-introduces the Hilbert-polynomial indexing
1439	via the direct images `E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)`.
1440	
1441	Blueprint references: `lem:flat_locus_open`, `lem:nonflat_locus_proper`,
1442	`lem:noetherian_induction_strata`. -/
1443	
1444	/-- **Lemma 5 (special case `n = 0`).** [Nitsure §4 special case]
1445	
1446	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` a coherent `𝓞_S`-module, there exists a
1447	countable family `S_e ⊆ S` (indexed by `e ∈ ℕ`) of locally-closed
1448	subschemes such that
1449	- each `S_e` is a (locally-closed) immersion `S_e ⟶ S`;
1450	- the underlying sets `|S_e|` partition `|S|`;
1451	- the pullback `𝓕|_{S_e}` is flat over `𝓞_{S_e}` — encoded as
1452	  `CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 (S_ e))`, i.e. flatness of the section modules of
1453	  the pulled-back sheaf over the section rings of the stratum *itself*.
1454	  (The *rank*-`e` locally-free refinement is future work once the
1455	  locally-free-of-rank-`e` predicate is in scope.)
1456	
1457	Statement repair (run 0010, T12 r2): the former conclusion asserted
1458	`CoherentSheafFlat (ι e)` — flatness of the pulled-back sheaf over the
1459	*ambient* `S` via the immersion — which is FALSE for any non-open stratum:
1460	a nonzero module supported on a closed stratum `V(x) ⊆ 𝔸¹` is killed by
1461	`x`, hence torsion, hence not flat over `𝓞_{𝔸¹}`-sections (e.g. the rank
1462	stratification of the skyscraper `k(0)` on `𝔸¹` refutes it). Nitsure's
1463	content is flatness (indeed local freeness) over the stratum, which is
1464	`CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 (S_ e))`.
1465	
1466	The function `s ↦ dim_{κ(s)} 𝓕|_s` is upper-semicontinuous (this is
1467	content (iii) of Nitsure's special case), encoded indirectly by the
1468	fact that the strata are locally-closed and disjoint.
1469	
1470	Nitsure's proof: Nakayama produces a local presentation
1471	`𝓞_V^{⊕m} ⟶ 𝓞_V^{⊕e} ⟶ 𝓕|_V ⟶ 0`; the closed subscheme `V_e ⊆ V`
1472	defined by the matrix-entry ideal of the first map represents the
1473	locus of locally-free-of-rank-`e`; gluing over a cover and intersecting
1474	with `V ∖ ⋃_{e' > e} V_{e'}` produces the locally-closed `S_e`. -/

1476	    (F : S.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
1477	    ∃ (S_ : ℕ → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ e, S_ e ⟶ S),
1478	      (∀ e, IsImmersion (ι e)) ∧
1479	      (∀ e e', e ≠ e' → Disjoint (Set.range (ι e).base) (Set.range (ι e').base)) ∧
1480	      (∀ s : S, ∃ e, s ∈ Set.range (ι e).base) ∧
1481	      (∀ e, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 (S_ e))
1482	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (ι e)).obj F)) := by

1485	/-- **Lemma 6 (Noetherian-induction reduction).** [Nitsure §4 general
1486	case opening]
1487	
1488	For `S` noetherian, `π : X ⟶ S` proper, and `𝓕` coherent on `X`, there
1489	exist finitely many reduced locally-closed immersions `V_i ⟶ S` with
1490	pairwise disjoint set-theoretic images covering `|S|`, such that the
1491	pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S V_i}` is flat over `V_i`.
1492	
1493	Nitsure's proof: peel off non-empty open flat patches by
1494	`genericFlatness` applied to each irreducible component (with its
1495	reduced subscheme structure), induct on the closed complement
1496	`S ∖ V` (which is again noetherian, hence the induction terminates by
1497	Noetherianity).
1498	
1499	The substantive content over the special case (Lemma 5) is that the
1500	strata `V_i` are *reduced* and that `𝓕` becomes flat above them (not
1501	merely that strata exist); the polynomial-indexed refinement of the
1502	main theorem (`flatteningStratification`) requires further assembly
1503	(`flatLocusAssembly`).
1504	
1505	Statement repair (run 0010, T12 r5): the hypothesis is `IsNoetherian S`
1506	(Nitsure's "noetherian"), not merely `IsLocallyNoetherian S` as the
1507	iter-176 skeleton had it.  With only local noetherianity the *finite*
1508	family is impossible: on `S = ⊔_{n≥1} 𝔸ⁿ` give component `n` the coherent
1509	sheaf `⊕_{k≤n} (i_{V_k})_* 𝓞_{V_k}` for a strictly nested flag
1510	`V_1 ⊃ ⋯ ⊃ V_n`; a flat locally-closed stratum through the generic point
1511	`η_k` of `V_k` cannot contain `η_{k'}` for `k' ≠ k` (near `η_{k'}` every

1516	statement below (`flatLocusAssembly`, `flatteningStratification`,

1520	lemma flatLocusReduction {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsNoetherian S]
1521	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
1522	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (V_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ i, V_ i ⟶ S),
1523	      (∀ i, IsImmersion (ι i)) ∧
1524	      (∀ i j, i ≠ j → Disjoint (Set.range (ι i).base) (Set.range (ι j).base)) ∧
1525	      (∀ s : S, ∃ i, s ∈ Set.range (ι i).base) ∧
1526	      (∀ i, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι i))
1527	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι i))).obj F)) := by

1530	/-- **Lemma 7 (assembly via direct images).** [Nitsure §4 assembly step]
1531	
1532	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` coherent on a proper `π : X ⟶ S`, there
1533	exists an integer `N` such that iterating the `n=0` stratification
1534	(Lemma 5) on the direct images `E_i := π_*𝓕(N+i)` on `S`
1535	(`i = 0, 1, …`) produces, after finitely many refinements, the
1536	Hilbert-polynomial-indexed stratification of the main theorem.
1537	
1538	The substantive content is the existence of the uniform vanishing
1539	bound `N` together with the finite refinement chain producing the
1540	locally-closed strata `S_f` from the rank-strata `W_{e_0, …, e_n}`.
1541	
1542	For the iter-176 file-skeleton this is encoded by the existence of a
1543	finite locally-closed stratification refining Lemma 6, together with
1544	the assertion that the strata carry constant Hilbert polynomial (the
1545	"polynomial-locally-constant" content of Nitsure's statement (A)).
1546	The Hilbert polynomial itself is encoded abstractly as a function
1547	`I → (ℕ → ℤ)` (each numerical polynomial restricted to `ℕ`); the
1548	substantive refinement to `numericalPolynomial` of degree `≤ n` is
1549	iter-177+ work.

1550	
1551	Proof status (run 0010, T12 r2): as typed, the `P`-conjunct only demands
1552	*some* injection `I → (ℕ → ℤ)`, which any finite index type admits; the
1553	statement is therefore a formal corollary of `flatLocusReduction`, and is
1554	proved as such below. The genuinely-Hilbert-indexed refinement must
1555	strengthen the statement (tie `P f` to fibre Euler characteristics), which
1556	awaits the coherent-χ substrate. -/
1557	lemma flatLocusAssembly {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsNoetherian S]
1558	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
1559	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S)
1560	      (P : I → ℕ → ℤ),
1561	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
1562	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
1563	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
1564	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι f))
1565	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) ∧
1566	      (∀ f g, f = g ↔ P f = P g) := by
1567	  obtain ⟨I, hI, V_, ι, himm, hdisj, hcov, hflat⟩ := flatLocusReduction π F
1568	  haveI := hI
1569	  haveI := Fintype.ofFinite I
1570	  refine ⟨I, hI, V_, ι, fun i _ => ((Fintype.equivFin I) i : ℤ), himm, hdisj, hcov, hflat,
1571	    fun f g => ⟨fun h => h ▸ rfl, fun h => ?_⟩⟩
1572	  have h0 : (((Fintype.equivFin I) f : ℕ) : ℤ) = (((Fintype.equivFin I) g : ℕ) : ℤ) :=
1573	    congrFun h 0
1574	  exact (Fintype.equivFin I).injective (Fin.val_injective (by exact_mod_cast h0))

1576	/-! ## §4. Existence of the flattening stratification (Nitsure §4 main)
1577	
1578	The main theorem combines the three sub-lemmas above: starting from a
1579	proper morphism `π : X ⟶ S` and a coherent sheaf `𝓕`, one obtains a
1580	finite locally-closed stratification of `S` indexed by Hilbert
1581	polynomials, on each piece of which `𝓕` becomes flat, with all four
1582	properties of Nitsure's Theorem packaged in a single conjunction.
1583	
1584	Blueprint reference: `thm:flattening_stratification_exists`
1585	(Nitsure §4; Stacks 052H). -/
1586	
1587	/-- **Flattening stratification existence theorem** [Nitsure §4 main /
1588	Stacks 052H].
1589	
1590	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a proper morphism `π : X ⟶ S`, and a
1591	coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a finite locally-closed
1592	stratification `{S_f}` of `S` indexed by a finite set `I` such that
1593	- each `ι : S_f ⟶ S` is a (locally-closed) immersion;
1594	- the underlying sets `|S_f|` partition `|S|` (disjoint and covering);
1595	- the pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S S_f}` is flat over `S_f` for each `f`.
1596	
1597	The (intended) substantive refinement is that the index set `I` is in
1598	bijection with the set of Hilbert polynomials arising on fibres, and
1599	that each `S_f` is uniquely determined by its Hilbert polynomial. For
1600	the iter-176 file-skeleton the substantive type captures the
1601	stratification + flatness; the Hilbert-polynomial labeling is encoded
1602	in `flatLocusAssembly`'s `P : I → ℕ → ℤ` injection but elided here for
1603	type-clarity.
1604	
1605	Proof status (run 0010, T12 r2): the conclusion as typed is exactly the
1606	conclusion of `flatLocusReduction` (Lemma 6) up to conjunct order, so the
1607	theorem is proved below by that reduction; the remaining mathematical
1608	content of this cone therefore lives in `flatLocusReduction` (Noetherian
1609	induction on `genericFlatness`) and `genericFlatness` itself. The
1610	Hilbert-polynomial-indexed refinement (which would need relative
1611	projective space `ℙⁿ_S`, Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity, direct-image
1612	base change 02KH) is deliberately not part of this statement; see
1613	`flatLocusAssembly` and the blueprint chapter §`Mathlib status`. -/
1614	theorem flatteningStratification {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsNoetherian S]
1615	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
1616	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S),
1617	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
1618	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
1619	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
1620	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι f))
1621	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) := by
1622	  obtain ⟨I, hI, V_, ι, himm, hdisj, hcov, hflat⟩ := flatLocusReduction π F
1623	  exact ⟨I, hI, V_, ι, himm, hcov, hdisj, hflat⟩
1624	
1625	/-! ## §5. Universal property of the stratification
1626	
1627	Nitsure's part (ii): the morphism `i : ⨿_f S_f → S` assembled from the
1628	stratum inclusions is universal for "`𝓕` becomes `T`-flat after pullback
1629	along `T → S`": any `φ : T ⟶ S` with `T`-flat pullback factors uniquely
1630	through the coproduct `∐ S_f`.
1631	
1632	Statement repair (run 0010, T12 r2): the former statement quantified over
1633	*arbitrary* finite immersion families `(I, S_, ι)` — for which the ∃!
1634	factorisation is FALSE (take `I = PEmpty`, or refine a genuine stratum
1635	into two disjoint pieces: covering/disjointness/flatness survive but a
1636	connected flat `T` straddling the two pieces no longer factors).
1637	Universality is a property of *the* flattening stratification produced by
1638	the construction (maximality of the strata), not of every disjoint flat
1639	family, so it cannot be hypothesised away from the existence statement.
1640	The repaired statement below therefore *bundles* existence and
1641	universality: there exists a stratification with the four structural
1642	properties of `flatteningStratification` AND the unique-factorisation
1643	property against every `T`-flat `φ`. The factorisation target is the
1644	scheme coproduct `∐ S_`, along `Sigma.desc ι : ∐ S_ ⟶ S` — the
1645	single-stratum ∃!-form is false for disconnected `T`, which can hit
1646	several strata.
1647	
1648	Blueprint reference: `thm:flattening_stratification_universal`
1649	(Nitsure §4 (ii); Stacks 052H). -/
1650	
1651	/-- **Universal property of the flattening stratification**
1652	[Nitsure §4 (ii) / Stacks 052H].
1653	
1654	There is a finite locally-closed stratification `{S_f}` of `S` — with the
1655	four structural properties of `flatteningStratification` (immersions,
1656	covering, disjoint, flat pullback on each stratum) — such that every
1657	`φ : T ⟶ S` whose pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S T}` is `T`-flat factors uniquely
1658	through `Sigma.desc ι : ∐ S_ ⟶ S`.
1659	
1660	(The converse direction — every morphism factoring through the coproduct
1661	has flat pullback — is also part of Nitsure's part (ii); it amounts to
1662	stability of `CoherentSheafFlat` under base change and should be added as
1663	a separate lemma when the base-change API for the predicate lands.)
1664	
1665	iter-177+: refine to a `Functor.RepresentableBy`-style universal arrow
1666	once the contravariant functor `T ↦ {φ : T ⟶ S | 𝓕|_{X_T} is T-flat}` is
1667	available. -/
1668	theorem flatteningStratification_universal {S X : Scheme.{u}}
1669	    [IsNoetherian S] (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules)
1670	    [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
1671	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S),
1672	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
1673	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
1674	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
1675	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι f))
1676	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) ∧
1677	      (∀ {T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T ⟶ S),
1678	        Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π φ)
1679	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π φ)).obj F) →
1680	        ∃! ψ : T ⟶ ∐ S_, ψ ≫ Sigma.desc ι = φ) := by
1681	  sorry
1682	
1683	/-! ## §6. Curve specialisation (Route A consumer)
1684	
1685	In the Route A consumer setting, the input morphism is the second
1686	projection `pr_T : C ×_k T ⟶ T` of the pullback of a smooth proper
1687	curve `C/k` against a noetherian `k`-scheme `T`. A smooth proper curve
1688	over a field is automatically projective (Hartshorne II.6.7 / II.7.6;
1689	folklore), hence in particular proper; the relative-curve `C ×_k T → T`
1690	inherits properness by base change, hence the hypotheses of the main
1691	theorem are met. This corollary records the specialisation
1692	explicitly. -/
1693	
1694	/-- **Flattening stratification for a coherent sheaf on a relative
1695	curve** [Nitsure §4 corollary; Route~A consumer A.2.a entry-point].
1696	
1697	Let `k` be a field, `C` a smooth proper curve over `k` (encoded as
1698	`C : Over (Spec k)` with `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]` and
1699	`[IsProper C.hom]`), and `T` a noetherian `k`-scheme. For any coherent
1700	sheaf `𝓕` on the relative curve `C ×_k T`, the flattening
1701	stratification conclusion of `flatteningStratification` applies to
1702	`π = pr_T : C ×_k T → T` and `𝓕`: there is a finite locally-closed
1703	stratification `{T_f ⊆ T}` set-theoretically covering `T` disjointly,
1704	such that `𝓕|_{C ×_k T_f}` is flat over `T_f` for each `f`.
1705	
1706	The body invokes `flatteningStratification` on the base-changed
1707	morphism `pullback.snd C.hom T.hom : (C ×_k T) ⟶ T`, using that
1708	`IsProper (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom)` holds by base change of
1709	`IsProper C.hom`; hence this corollary is proved (no `sorry`). -/
1710	theorem flatteningStratification.ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k]
1711	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
1712	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
1713	    (T : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsNoetherian T.left]
1714	    (F : (Limits.pullback C.hom T.hom).Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
1715	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (T_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, T_ f ⟶ T.left),
1716	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
1717	      (∀ t : T.left, ∃ f, t ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
1718	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
1719	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat
1720	        (pullback.snd (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))
1721	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
1722	          (pullback.fst (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))).obj F)) :=
1723	  flatteningStratification (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) F
1724	
1725	end AlgebraicGeometry

408	/-! ## §2. Generic flatness (Nitsure §4)
409	
410	Over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type
411	`X ⟶ S` is flat above some non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. This is the inductive
412	engine of the flattening-stratification theorem: combined with
413	Noetherian induction on the closed complement `S ∖ V`, it produces the
414	finite stratification of `S` by flatness loci.
415	
416	Algebraically (theorem `generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin): for a
417	noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
418	`B`-module `M`, there exists a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is a
419	free `A_f`-module. The geometric form (this declaration) restricts to a
420	non-empty affine open `Spec A ⊆ S` and applies the algebraic form on
421	each finite-type-algebra patch of `X` above `Spec A`.
422	
423	Blueprint reference: `thm:generic_flatness` (Nitsure §4). -/
424	
425	/-- **Generic flatness theorem** (Nitsure §4 / Stacks ?).
426	
427	For a noetherian integral scheme `S`, a finite-type morphism `p : X ⟶ S`,
428	and a coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a non-empty open subscheme
429	`V ⊆ S` such that `𝓕|_{X_V} = 𝓕|_{p⁻¹V}` is flat over `𝓞_V`.
430	
431	PROVED (run 0010, T12 r4), following Nitsure §4: pass to a non-empty affine
432	open `U₀ ⊆ S` with `A := Γ(S, U₀)` a noetherian domain, cover the compact
433	preimage `p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀` by finitely many affine charts with finitely generated
434	sections (`Scheme.Modules.exists_affine_finite_sections_nhds`, Stacks 01PC),
435	apply algebraic generic freeness (`GenericFreeness.genericFlatnessAlgebraic`)
436	on each chart, and take `f ∈ A` the product of the witnesses.  The witness
437	open is `V := D(f)`, non-empty since `A` is a domain and `f ≠ 0`; the
438	quantified flatness on affine pairs below `V` is `flat_section_pair` (§1b).
439	
440	Statement repair (run 0010, T12 r2): Nitsure requires `p` of **finite type**
441	(EGA sense: quasi-compact AND locally of finite type). With
442	`LocallyOfFiniteType` alone the statement is FALSE: for
443	`X = ⨿_q Spec 𝔽_q → Spec ℤ` (locally of finite type, not quasi-compact,
444	structure sheaf finitely presented) every non-empty open `V ⊆ Spec ℤ`
445	contains all but finitely many primes `q`, and `𝔽_q` is never flat over the
446	corresponding `ℤ[1/n]`. Quasi-compactness is what bounds the number of
447	denominators to clear. Hence the `[QuasiCompact p]` hypothesis below. -/
448	theorem genericFlatness {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral S] [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
449	    (p : X ⟶ S) [QuasiCompact p] [LocallyOfFiniteType p] (F : X.Modules)
450	    [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
451	    ∃ (V : S.Opens), (V : Set S).Nonempty ∧
452	      ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (_ : U ≤ V) {W : X.Opens}
453	        (_ : IsAffineOpen W) (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U),
454	        letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
455	        Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := by
456	  classical
457	  -- an ambient nonempty affine chart of the integral base
458	  obtain ⟨x₀⟩ : Nonempty S := inferInstance
459	  obtain ⟨_, ⟨U₀, hU₀, rfl⟩, hx₀U₀, -⟩ :=
460	    S.isBasis_affineOpens.exists_subset_of_mem_open (Set.mem_univ x₀) isOpen_univ
461	  haveI : IsDomain Γ(S, U₀) := @IsIntegral.component_integral _ _ _ ⟨⟨x₀, hx₀U₀⟩⟩
462	  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Γ(S, U₀) :=
463	    IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian ⟨U₀, hU₀⟩
464	  -- affine charts with finitely generated sections covering the compact preimage
465	  have hK : IsCompact ((p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ : X.Opens) : Set X) :=
466	    p.isCompact_preimage hU₀.isCompact
467	  have HX : ∀ x : X, x ∈ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ → ∃ V : X.Opens, IsAffineOpen V ∧ x ∈ V ∧
468	      V ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ ∧ Module.Finite Γ(X, V) Γ(F, V) := fun x hx =>
469	    Scheme.Modules.exists_affine_finite_sections_nhds F x (p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀) hx
470	  choose! V hVaff hxV hVle hVfin using HX
471	  obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := hK.elim_finite_subcover
472	    (fun x : ↥(p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ : X.Opens) => ((V x.1 : X.Opens) : Set X))
473	    (fun x => (V x.1).2)
474	    (fun y hy => Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ⟨⟨y, hy⟩, hxV y hy⟩)
475	  -- per-chart generic freeness over `A := Γ(S, U₀)`
476	  have hGF : ∀ i : ↥t, ∃ fi : Γ(S, U₀), fi ≠ 0 ∧
477	      (letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, V i.1.1) :=
478	        Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (V i.1.1) (hVle i.1.1 i.1.2)).hom
479	      Module.Free (Localization.Away fi)
480	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers fi) Γ(F, V i.1.1))) := by
481	    intro i
482	    letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, V i.1.1) :=
483	      Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (V i.1.1) (hVle i.1.1 i.1.2)).hom
484	    letI : Algebra Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, V i.1.1) :=
485	      (p.appLE U₀ (V i.1.1) (hVle i.1.1 i.1.2)).hom.toAlgebra
486	    haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, V i.1.1) Γ(F, V i.1.1) :=
487	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
488	    haveI hft : Algebra.FiniteType Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, V i.1.1) :=
489	      HasRingHomProperty.appLE (P := @LocallyOfFiniteType) p ‹_› ⟨U₀, hU₀⟩
490	        ⟨V i.1.1, hVaff i.1.1 i.1.2⟩ (hVle i.1.1 i.1.2)
491	    haveI := hVfin i.1.1 i.1.2
492	    exact GenericFreeness.genericFlatnessAlgebraic Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, V i.1.1)
493	      Γ(F, V i.1.1)
494	  choose ff hff0 hffree using hGF
495	  -- the common denominator
496	  set f : Γ(S, U₀) := ∏ i ∈ (Finset.univ : Finset ↥t), ff i with hfdef
497	  have hf0 : f ≠ 0 := by
498	    rw [hfdef]
499	    exact Finset.prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr fun i _ => hff0 i
500	  refine ⟨S.basicOpen f, ?_, ?_⟩
501	  · -- non-emptiness: on a reduced scheme `D(f) = ⊥` forces `f = 0`
502	    rw [← TopologicalSpace.Opens.ne_bot_iff_nonempty]
503	    intro hbot
504	    exact hf0 ((basicOpen_eq_bot_iff f).mp hbot)
505	  · -- flatness on every affine pair below `D(f)`, via the two-layer reduction
506	    intro U hU hUf W hW e
507	    refine flat_section_pair p F hU₀ (fun i : ↥t => V i.1.1)
508	      (fun i => hVaff i.1.1 i.1.2) (fun i => hVle i.1.1 i.1.2) ?_ f ?_ hU hUf hW e
509	    · -- the charts cover `p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀`
510	      intro x hx
511	      have := ht hx
512	      rw [Set.mem_iUnion₂] at this
513	      obtain ⟨i, hi, hxi⟩ := this
514	      exact ⟨⟨i, hi⟩, hxi⟩
515	    · -- freeness at the common denominator, chart by chart
516	      intro i
517	      letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, V i.1.1) :=
518	        Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (V i.1.1) (hVle i.1.1 i.1.2)).hom
519	      have h1 := GenericFreeness.free_localizedModule_powers_mul Γ(S, U₀)
520	        Γ(F, V i.1.1) (ff i) (∏ j ∈ Finset.univ.erase i, ff j) (hffree i)
521	      rwa [Finset.mul_prod_erase _ _ (Finset.mem_univ i)] at h1
522

data.content:
/-
Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Christian Merten
-/
import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.FlatteningStratification
import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.QcohTildeSections

/-!
# Generic flatness: the geometric statement (Nitsure §4)

This file proves the **geometric generic flatness theorem**
`AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness` (blueprint node `thm:generic_flatness`,
Nitsure §4): over an integral locally noetherian base `S`, a finitely
presented module sheaf on a finite-type (quasi-compact and locally of finite
type) `X ⟶ S` has flat sections over all affine pairs below some non-empty
open `V ⊆ S`.

It is the geometric-glue layer on top of the algebraic generic-freeness
engine of `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.FlatteningStratification`
(`GenericFreeness.genericFlatnessAlgebraic`, Stacks 051R).  The declaration
lives in its own file — rather than in `FlatteningStratification.lean`, whose
remaining statements it logically belongs with — because the glue needs the
qcqs section-localization engine of `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme`
(Stacks 01P0/01PC/01I8) and the base-ring descent of
`AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.QcohTildeSections`, and importing those files
into `FlatteningStratification.lean` perturbs the instance environment of its
(heavy, finished) dévissage proofs.
=====
1:/-
6:import Mathlib
8:/-!
31:one, imports the QuotScheme engine — keeping the instance environment of the
=====
AlgebraicJacobian.lean:77:import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.FlatteningStratification
AlgebraicJacobian.lean:78:import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GenericFlatnessGeometric
AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean:7:import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.FlatteningStratification

84	section GenericFlatnessGeometricGlue
85	
86	/-- Elementwise fibre-side `appLE` coherence: restricting the image of `appLE`
87	equals `appLE` into the smaller open. -/
88	private lemma appLE_res_apply {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) {U : S.Opens}
89	    {W W' : X.Opens} (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U) (h : W' ≤ W) (u : Γ(S, U)) :
90	    (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom ((p.appLE U W e).hom u) =
91	      (p.appLE U W' (h.trans e)).hom u := by
92	  have h1 := congrArg (fun (φ : Γ(S, U) ⟶ Γ(X, W')) => φ.hom u)
93	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE_map (f := p) e (homOfLE h).op)
94	  simpa only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply] using h1
95	
96	/-- Elementwise base-side `appLE` coherence: `appLE` of a restricted base section
97	equals `appLE` from the larger base open. -/
98	private lemma appLE_base_res_apply {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) {U U' : S.Opens}
99	    {W : X.Opens} (h : U' ≤ U) (e' : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U') (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U) (u : Γ(S, U)) :
100	    (p.appLE U' W e').hom ((S.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom u) =
101	      (p.appLE U W e).hom u := by
102	  have h1 := congrArg (fun (φ : Γ(S, U) ⟶ Γ(X, W)) => φ.hom u)
103	    (Scheme.Hom.map_appLE (f := p) e' (homOfLE h).op)
104	  simpa only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply] using h1
105	
106	/-- Transport of section flatness along an equality of opens: the `Module.compHom`
107	structures via `appLE` agree definitionally because the `≤`-witness of `appLE` is
108	proof-irrelevant. -/
109	private theorem flat_sections_congr {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules)
110	    {U₀ : S.Opens} {O₁ O₂ : X.Opens} (hEq : O₁ = O₂)
111	    (e₁ : O₁ ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀) (e₂ : O₂ ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀)
112	    (h : letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₁) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ O₁ e₁).hom
113	         Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₁)) :
114	    letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₂) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ O₂ e₂).hom
115	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₂) := by
116	  subst hEq
117	  exact h
118	
119	open TensorProduct in
120	/-- **Mixed-base stability of flatness under module localization.**  Let `B` be an
121	`R`-algebra, `N` a `B`-module that is flat over `R`, and `g : N →ₗ[B] N'` a
122	localization of `N` at a submonoid `p ⊆ B`.  Then `N'` is still flat over `R`.
123	
124	Mathlib's `Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule` treats only submonoids of `R`
125	itself; here the localization happens on the fibre side.  The proof rewrites
126	`𝟙 N' ⊗ f` as the `IsLocalizedModule.map` of `𝟙 N ⊗ f` (Mathlib's
127	`IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor`, with the `IsLocalizedModule.rTensor` instances)
128	and uses that localization preserves injectivity
129	(`IsLocalizedModule.map_injective`). -/
130	theorem _root_.Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule_algebra {R B : Type v} [CommRing R]
131	    [CommRing B] [Algebra R B] (p : Submonoid B) {N N' : Type v} [AddCommGroup N]
132	    [Module R N] [Module B N] [IsScalarTower R B N] [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N']
133	    [Module B N'] [IsScalarTower R B N'] (g : N →ₗ[B] N') [IsLocalizedModule p g]
134	    [Module.Flat R N] : Module.Flat R N' := by
135	  rw [Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ]
136	  intro P _ _ I
137	  rw [← TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor (A := B)]
138	  rw [← IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor (S := p) (g := g) (f := I.subtype)]
139	  exact IsLocalizedModule.map_injective p
140	    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R I g)
141	    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R P g)
142	    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.lTensor B N I.subtype)
143	    (by
144	      rw [TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor (A := B)]
145	      exact Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap I.subtype
146	        Subtype.val_injective)
147	
148	set_option maxHeartbeats 1200000 in
149	-- Heartbeat headroom: several `IsLocalizedModule`/scalar-tower instance chains
150	-- at localized section modules (qcqs section-localization engine).
151	/-- **Chart-basic core of generic flatness.**  Let `Wj ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀` be an affine
152	chart whose section module localized at `g ∈ Γ(S, U₀)` is free over
153	`Γ(S, U₀)_g` (the output of algebraic generic freeness), and let `c ∈ Γ(X, Wj)`
154	cut a basic open lying above the base basic open `D(g)`.  Then the sections of
155	`F` over `D(c)` (presented as any open `O = D(c)`) are flat over `Γ(S, U₀)`.
156	
157	Route: `D(β) = Wj ⊓ p ⁻¹ᵁ D(g)` for `β := appLE g`; the section restriction
158	`Γ(F, Wj) → Γ(F, D(β))` is a localization at `powers β` over `Γ(X, Wj)`
159	(Stacks 01P0) hence at `powers g` over `Γ(S, U₀)` (base-ring descent), so
160	`Γ(F, D(β))` inherits freeness-hence-flatness over `Γ(S, U₀)` from the
161	hypothesis; finally `Γ(F, D(β)) → Γ(F, D(c))` is a fibre-side localization,
162	absorbed by mixed-base stability. -/
163	private theorem flat_section_chartBasic {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S)
164	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] {U₀ : S.Opens}
165	    {Wj : X.Opens} (hWj : IsAffineOpen Wj) (eWj : Wj ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀) (g : Γ(S, U₀))
166	    (hfree :
167	      letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, Wj) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom
168	      Module.Free (Localization.Away g)
169	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj)))
170	    (c : Γ(X, Wj)) (hc : X.basicOpen c ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ (S.basicOpen g))
171	    {O : X.Opens} (hO : O = X.basicOpen c) (eO : O ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀) :
172	    letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ O eO).hom
173	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O) := by
174	  subst hO
175	  letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, Wj) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom
176	  -- the base element pushed into the chart ring, and its basic open
177	  set β : Γ(X, Wj) := (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom g with hβ
178	  have hDβ : X.basicOpen β = Wj ⊓ p ⁻¹ᵁ (S.basicOpen g) := by
179	    rw [hβ, show (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom g
180	        = (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE eWj).op).hom ((p.app U₀).hom g) from rfl]
181	    rw [Scheme.basicOpen_res, Scheme.preimage_basicOpen]
182	  have hDcβ : X.basicOpen c ≤ X.basicOpen β := by
183	    rw [hDβ]
184	    exact le_inf (X.basicOpen_le c) hc
185	  have eβ : X.basicOpen β ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ := (X.basicOpen_le β).trans eWj
186	  -- (1) chart-side localization of sections at β (Stacks 01P0)
187	  letI : Module Γ(X, Wj) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
188	    Module.compHom _ (algebraMap Γ(X, Wj) Γ(X, X.basicOpen β))
189	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(X, Wj) Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
190	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
191	  haveI hlocB : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers β)
192	      (Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F β) :=
193	    Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isCompact F hWj.isCompact
194	      hWj.isQuasiSeparated β
195	  -- (2) `Γ(S, U₀)`-module structures and scalar towers
196	  letI : Algebra Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, Wj) := (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom.toAlgebra
197	  letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
198	    Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen β) eβ).hom
199	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, Wj) Γ(F, Wj) :=
200	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
201	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, Wj) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
202	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun a n => by
203	      change (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le β)).op).hom
204	          ((p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom a) • n
205	        = (p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen β) eβ).hom a • n
206	      rw [appLE_res_apply p eWj (X.basicOpen_le β) a])
207	  -- (3) base-ring descent of the localization, and flatness of `Γ(F, D(β))`
208	  haveI hlocA : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g)
209	      ((Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F β).restrictScalars Γ(S, U₀)) := by
210	    refine isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap g _ ?_
211	    exact hlocB
212	  haveI hfreeg : Module.Free (Localization.Away g)
213	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj)) := hfree
214	  haveI hflatLoc : Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀)
215	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj)) := by
216	    haveI : Module.Flat (Localization.Away g)
217	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj)) := inferInstance
218	    exact (Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization (Localization.Away g)
219	      (Submonoid.powers g) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj))).mp this
220	  haveI hflatNβ : Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
221	    Module.Flat.of_linearEquiv
222	      (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers g)
223	        ((Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F β).restrictScalars Γ(S, U₀))).symm
224	  -- (4) restrict from `D(β)` to `D(c)` and absorb by mixed-base stability
225	  set c' : Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) :=
226	    (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le β)).op).hom c with hc'
227	  have hDc' : X.basicOpen c' = X.basicOpen c := by
228	    rw [hc', Scheme.basicOpen_res]
229	    exact inf_eq_right.mpr hDcβ
230	  have ec' : X.basicOpen c' ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ := (X.basicOpen_le c').trans eβ
231	  letI : Module Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(F, X.basicOpen c') :=
232	    Module.compHom _ (algebraMap Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(X, X.basicOpen c'))
233	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(X, X.basicOpen c')
234	      Γ(F, X.basicOpen c') :=
235	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
236	  haveI hlocC : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers c')
237	      (Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F c') :=
238	    Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isCompact F
239	      (hWj.basicOpen β).isCompact (hWj.basicOpen β).isQuasiSeparated c'
240	  letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, X.basicOpen c') :=
241	    Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen c') ec').hom
242	  letI : Algebra Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) :=
243	    (p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen β) eβ).hom.toAlgebra
244	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
245	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
246	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(F, X.basicOpen c') :=
247	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun a n => by
248	      change (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le c')).op).hom
249	          ((p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen β) eβ).hom a) • n
250	        = (p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen c') ec').hom a • n
251	      rw [appLE_res_apply p eβ (X.basicOpen_le c') a])
252	  haveI hflatC' : Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, X.basicOpen c') :=
253	    Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule_algebra (Submonoid.powers c')
254	      (Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F c')
255	  exact flat_sections_congr p F hDc' ec' eO hflatC'
256	
257	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
258	-- Heartbeat headroom: per-piece instance provisioning under binders for the
259	-- `flat_of_isLocalized_span` application.
260	/-- **Two-layer basic-open reduction for generic flatness.**  Given affine charts
261	`Wc j ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀` covering `p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀`, all of whose section modules localized
262	at `f ∈ Γ(S, U₀)` are free (the output of algebraic generic freeness at a
263	common denominator `f`), every affine pair `U ≤ D(f)`, `W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U` has flat
264	sections: cover `W` by opens simultaneously basic in `W` and in a chart, lying
265	above base basic opens common to `U` and `U₀`; conclude with

267	`Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization` exchanges on the base. -/

data.content:
/-
Copyright (c) 2026 Thomas Browning, Andrew Yang. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Thomas Browning, Andrew Yang
-/
module

public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.ClosedImmersion
public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Noetherian

/-!
# Subscheme structure on an irreducible component

We define the subscheme structure on an irreducible component of a Noetherian scheme. Typically,
one takes the reduced induced subscheme structure, but this will throw away information if the
irreducible component is not already reduced. Instead, we take the closed subscheme defined by
the kernel of the restriction to the complement of the union of the other irreducible components.
For example, if `X` is irreducible then this will give back the original scheme `X`.

## Main definition
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.irreducibleComponentIdeal`: The ideal sheaf data associated to an
  irreducible component of a Noetherian scheme.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.irreducibleComponent`: The subscheme structure on an irreducible
  component of a Noetherian scheme.

## TODO

Prove that for affine schemes this subscheme structure is defined by the kernel of the
localization away from the union of the other minimal prime ideals.

-/

@[expose] public section

universe u

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

variable (X : Scheme.{u}) (Z : Set X) (hZ : Z ∈ irreducibleComponents X) [IsNoetherian X]

/-- The complement of the irreducible components unequal to `Z` of a Noetherian scheme. -/
def irreducibleComponentOpen : Opens X :=
  ⟨(⋃₀ (irreducibleComponents X \ {Z}))ᶜ, by
    rw [Set.sUnion_eq_biUnion, isOpen_compl_iff]
    exact TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents.sdiff.isClosed_biUnion
      fun W hW ↦ isClosed_of_mem_irreducibleComponents W hW.1⟩

/-- The ideal sheaf data associated to an irreducible component of a Noetherian scheme. -/
def irreducibleComponentIdeal : X.IdealSheafData where
  __ := (irreducibleComponentOpen X Z).ι.ker
  supportSet := Z
  supportSet_eq_iInter_zeroLocus := by
    rw [← IdealSheafData.coe_support_eq_eq_iInter_zeroLocus, Hom.support_ker, Opens.range_ι]
    exact (closure_sUnion_irreducibleComponents_sdiff_singleton
      TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents Z hZ).symm

theorem irreducibleComponentIdeal_def :
    irreducibleComponentIdeal X Z hZ = (irreducibleComponentOpen X Z).ι.ker := by
  ext
  rfl

/-- The subscheme structure on an irreducible component of a Noetherian scheme. -/
noncomputable def irreducibleComponent : Scheme :=
  (X.irreducibleComponentIdeal Z hZ).subscheme

/-- The inclusion from an irreducible component of a Noetherian scheme. -/
noncomputable def irreducibleComponentι : X.irreducibleComponent Z hZ ⟶ X :=
  (X.irreducibleComponentIdeal Z hZ).subschemeι

lemma irreducibleComponentι_apply (x : X.irreducibleComponent Z hZ) :
    X.irreducibleComponentι Z hZ x = x.1 :=
  rfl

instance : IsClosedImmersion (X.irreducibleComponentι Z hZ) :=
  inferInstanceAs (IsClosedImmersion (X.irreducibleComponentIdeal Z hZ).subschemeι)

instance : IrreducibleSpace (X.irreducibleComponent Z hZ) :=
  Subtype.irreducibleSpace hZ.1

include hZ in
theorem irreducibleComponentOpen_eq_top [IrreducibleSpace X] :
    irreducibleComponentOpen X Z = ⊤ := by
  rw [irreducibleComponents_eq_singleton, Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hZ
  simp [irreducibleComponentOpen, irreducibleComponents_eq_singleton, hZ]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
instance [IrreducibleSpace X] : CategoryTheory.IsIso (X.irreducibleComponentι Z hZ) := by
  have : CategoryTheory.IsIso (irreducibleComponentOpen X Z).ι := by
    rw [irreducibleComponentOpen_eq_top X Z hZ]
    exact X.topIso.isIso_hom
  rw [irreducibleComponentι, isIso_subschemeι_iff_eq_bot, irreducibleComponentIdeal_def,
    irreducibleComponentOpen_eq_top X Z hZ]
  exact X.topIso.hom.ker_eq_bot_of_isIso

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
